All I Ching FAQ
I Ching Reading Basics

What should I ask before an I Ching reading?

Ask one clear question about a real situation. The best I Ching questions invite reflection, timing, and practical review instead of demanding a guaranteed prediction.

DirectoryShort answerGood question patternsConcrete exampleQuestions to avoid

Short answer

Before an I Ching reading, ask one specific question that you can connect to real life. Good questions begin with what, how, where, or what should I notice. Weak questions demand certainty, try to control another person, or combine several issues at once. The clearer the question, the easier it is to interpret the hexagram responsibly.

Good question patterns

Useful questions ask about attention and response. For example: what should I review before accepting this offer, what needs care in this relationship conversation, what is the wise pace for this project, or what risk should I notice before spending money? These questions leave room for reflection while staying specific enough to act on.

Concrete example

Instead of asking Will I be successful? ask What should I pay attention to before launching this project? The second question can connect to timing, preparation, support, conflict, or restraint. When the result appears, you can compare it with real tasks, conversations, and evidence. That makes the reading useful without turning it into a promise.

Questions to avoid

Avoid asking for medical diagnoses, legal outcomes, financial guarantees, private information about another person, or emergency guidance. Also avoid asking the same question repeatedly because the first result was uncomfortable. If the answer feels unclear, improve the question, read the guide pages, and return when the situation has changed.
